# getrequest

getrequest is the API Reliability Layer for modern engineering teams.

It sits in front of your backend as a protective layer — relaying every
incoming request safely, retaining every payload before it reaches your
infrastructure, and allowing recovery from failures with exact-payload
replay. No code changes required.

## Product

- Category: API infrastructure, developer tools
- Positioning: The API Reliability Layer for modern teams
- Tagline: Relay safely. Retain durably. Recover instantly.

## Three stages of API reliability

- Relay — every request passes through a controlled layer before hitting
  your backend; protects against traffic spikes and cascading failures
- Retain — every request captured in full before reaching your
  infrastructure; zero request loss during downtime or deployment failures
- Recover — replay any retained request with exact original headers, body,
  and method; confirm fixes before shipping (Pro plan)

## Key capabilities

- Instant endpoints: Launch a production-ready API endpoint in under 60
  seconds (mock JSON or proxy to any upstream)
- Live inspector: Every request captured — headers, body, status, latency —
  searchable up to 30 days
- Request replay: Re-fire any captured request with original payload intact
  (Pro)
- Shareable debug links: Permanent URL to any captured request, viewable
  without an account (Pro)
- Smart forwarding: Transparent proxy to any upstream API, full exchange
  logged, zero code changes
- Usage metering: Request volume tracking, pre-limit alerts, audit trail

## Pricing

- Free: $0/mo — 10,000 requests/month, 7-day log retention, Relay + Retain
- Dev: $9/mo — 100,000 requests/month, 30-day log retention, Relay + Retain
- Pro: $99/mo — 1,000,000 requests/month, 30-day retention, full Relay +
  Retain + Recover (request replay, shareable debug links)

## Pages

- / — Homepage and product overview
- /features — Full feature documentation (Relay / Retain / Recover stages)
- /pricing — Pricing plans and comparison
- /use-cases — Use case scenarios for different team types
- /blog — Technical articles on API reliability, infrastructure, and
  developer tooling
